operating_state: Netflix generated $10.5 billion in revenue for Q1 2025, representing 13% growth year-over-year or 16% on constant currency basis, driven by broad-based global membership expansion and pricing optimization across all regions. Operating income reached $3.3 billion with operating margin expanding 360 basis points to 31.7% as revenue growth significantly outpaced expense increases.
cost_structure: Gross margin improved 300 basis points to 50% with content amortization rising reflecting expanded content slate investments. Operating expenses grew across technology and development from personnel expansion, sales and marketing from advertising sales headcount growth, and general administrative expenses from higher third-party costs.
material_events: The company repurchased $3.5 billion of common stock during the quarter, retiring 3.7 million shares under its expanded authorization program with $13.6 billion remaining available. Netflix repaid $800 million of maturing Senior Notes in February 2025 and discontinued quarterly reporting of membership metrics, focusing instead on revenue and operating margin as primary performance indicators.
macro_exposures: Foreign currency headwinds reduced revenue growth by approximately 300 basis points despite active hedging programs, with $36 million in foreign exchange losses compared to prior year gains primarily from euro-denominated debt remeasurement. The company maintains substantial exposure to multiple currencies representing 55% of revenue and 29% of operating expenses, with euro-denominated debt and derivative hedging contracts creating ongoing translation risk.
forward_outlook: Management continues targeting global business growth within operating margin parameters while investing significantly in original content requiring upfront cash payments well in advance of amortization. The company anticipates potential additional debt capital raises for refinancing upcoming maturities or strategic acquisitions, with capital allocation focused on content investment, share repurchases, and maintaining financial flexibility.
uncertainty_profile: Content obligations total $21.8 billion with significant portions due within twelve months plus estimated future title obligations under output deals and series agreements. Debt maturities include near-term principal and interest obligations with refinancing dependent on market conditions, while Brazilian tax authorities continue assessments with potential exposure and the company may face substantial tax deposit requirements.
